content.full_text:
0u i':
Ms. Carol Thorp has asked me to direct a hypothetical question to you for your
interpretation and response.
Hypothetical Situation :
At some future time a candidate for a local political office (Councilmember for
example), while on an out of state business trip interrupts the business trip and travels
from an out of state point of origin to his district, for the sole purpose of attending a
political forum, and then retums to the out of state point of origin inorder to resume the
business trip.
Questions:
Could the travel expenses related to the non-business, politically related portion
of the trip (travel from out of state location to district and return to out of state
location) be properly claimed as an authorized campaign travel expenditure on Form
490, Schedule E?
Would it be a violation of FPPC requirements if the politically related travel
expenses incurred, in the above example, were not reported or disclosed?
I am a candidate for Pleasanton Councilmember in the upcoming November 3,
1992 election and would appreciate a timely response so that future campaign
expenditures and reports could be timely and conectly filed.
Sincereiy yours,
--;\ 
n .
I ^ 
t,/
\Dw\ 1 ta
Thomas P. Pico, Jr.
795 Neal Place
Pleasanton, CA 94566
510-462-9484 (H)
510-462-0919 (w)
cc: Carol Thorp
Mr. John Wallace
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814
Dear Mr. Wallace:
"t-
, _ os ril ,g?
